description: You are the communications officer on a research vessel preparing for a North Atlantic expedition. Update the GPredict configuration by deleting the stale ground station 'PreviousCruise'. Create a ground station for your departure port named 'Woods_Hole' (41.5243 N, 70.6693 W, Altitude: 2m) and another for your research area named 'MidAtlantic' (42.0000 N, 30.0000 W, Altitude: 0m). Configure a tracking module named 'WX_Reception' containing NOAA 15 (NORAD 25338), NOAA 18 (NORAD 28654), and NOAA 19 (NORAD 33591), set to the 'Woods_Hole' ground station. Create a second module named 'SatComm' containing ISS (NORAD 25544), SO-50 (NORAD 27607), and AO-73 (NORAD 39444), set to the 'MidAtlantic' ground station. Do not modify the existing 'Amateur' module. Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

natural_language.prompt: I'm the chief communications officer on the R/V Atlantic Explorer. We're heading out to the Mid-Atlantic Ridge from Woods Hole, but this laptop still has the GPredict config from a Caribbean cruise. Please delete the 'PreviousCruise' ground station. Then set up two new ground stations: one for 'Woods_Hole' (41.5243 N, 70.6693 W, 2m altitude) and one for 'MidAtlantic' (42.0000 N, 30.0000 W, sea level/0m altitude). Next, I need two new tracking modules. Make one called 'WX_Reception' with NOAA 15, 18, and 19 (NORADs 25338, 28654, 33591) and lock its ground station to Woods_Hole so I can test the antennas before we leave port. Make another module called 'SatComm' with the ISS, SO-50, and AO-73 (NORADs 25544, 27607, 39444) and lock its ground station to the MidAtlantic waypoint for use during the cruise. Don't touch the default Amateur module.
